summary: Traverses the input's reachable own enumerable object-valued properties, runs a compaction step on the collected references, and returns the original input.
signature_intent: Accepts any JavaScript value and returns the same value reference after traversal and compaction.
behavior: Enumerates own enumerable string-keyed properties of each visited object.
Follows property values only when they are non-null objects.
Avoids revisiting the same discovered object reference more than once.
Invokes an external compaction step on the collected traversal records before returning.
Returns the original input unchanged by direct assignment.
edge_cases: Throws when the initial input is null or undefined.
Primitive inputs are coerced during property enumeration and usually produce no traversal.
Arrays are treated as ordinary objects with enumerable keys.
Functions are not followed unless encountered as enumerable containers through object-valued properties.
Cyclic object graphs terminate because repeated object references are skipped.
forbidden_details: No original identifier names, repository names, source paths, or unnecessary implementation quirks are included.
